content.problemStatement:
Write a C++ program that takes two integers and an operator (+, -, *, /) as input.

Use a 'switch' statement to perform the corresponding arithmetic operation and print the result.

Check the sample input / output below for clarity.

(**Note:** It is guaranteed that in the hidden test cases, the second number will not be 0 during division.)
constraints: op is a character; only '+', '-', '*', '/' are considered valid
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two integers num1 and num2, and a character op, separated by spaces.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the result of the arithmetic operation or 'Invalid operator' if op is not one of '+', '-', '*', '/'.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Three test cases are processed. 5+2=7, 10/10=1 (integer division), 7*3=21.
The operator '%' is not among the allowed symbols, so the program prints the error message.
Integer division 9/2 truncates toward zero, yielding 4.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by using a switch statement to determine which arithmetic operation to perform based on the input character op. The result of the operation is then printed to the console. If op is not one of the valid characters, 'Invalid operator' is printed.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) per test case, O(T) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — the space used does not grow with the size of the input.
